REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO CONSOLIDATE DISCOVERY IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO CITE A SINGLE AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT DISCOVERY MAY BE CONSOLIDATED IN THE CASES WHERE DISCOVERY HAS BEEN CLOSED IN ONE ACTION AND IS STILL OPEN IN THE OTHER. IN FACT, PLAINTIFF DOESN'T CITE A SINGLE CASE WHERE CONSOLIDATION FOR DISCOVERY PURPOSES ONLY WAS NOT MERELY MENTIONED IN DICTA/PROCEDURAL HISTORY RECITATION OF THE CASE. THE COURT NOTES THAT IT DOES NOT HAVE ACCESS TO O'CONNOR'S CALIFORNIA PRACTICE (2023 ED.) CIVIL PRETRIAL, CH. 5-H 4.1; PLAINTIFF DID NOT PROVIDE A COPY OF THIS SECONDARY SOURCE AND IT IS NOT CLEAR IF ANY CASES SUPPORTING PLAINTIFF'S POSITION ARE CITED THEREIN. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES, THAT IF THESE MATTERS WERE TO BE CONSOLIDATED FOR DISCOVERY ONLY, DISCOVERY WOULD TRACK ACTION 602059 AND WOULD BE CLOSED IN BOTH CASES AS OF OCTOBER 9, 2023. THIS DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE THE OUTCOME SOUGHT BY THE MOVING PARTY. THE PARTIES ARE WELCOME TO STIPULATE TO THE USE OF DISCOVERY OBTAINED IN EITHER CASE TO BE USED IN THE OTHER MATTER IN ORDER TO PRESERVE RESOURCES. THE COURT WILL NOT BE ADDRESSING ANY SUCH STIPULATION ON THIS MOTION AS IT IS NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT. LATE-FILED OPPOSITION AND SUR-REPLY ARE NOT CONSIDERED AND ARE STRICKEN. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2024 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-04-2024 Continued To Mar-11-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Consolidate Discovery
Off Calendar Nov-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF LIGHTSPEED INVESTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Consolidate Discovery is DENIED. Moving party fails to cite a single authority for the proposition that discovery may be consolidated in the cases where discovery has been closed in one action and is still open in the other. In fact, Plaintiff doesn't cite a single case where consolidation for discovery purposes only was not merely mentioned in dicta/procedural history recitation of the case. The Court notes that it does not have access to O'Connor's California Practice (2023 ed.) Civil Pretrial, Ch. 5-H 4.1; Plaintiff did not provide a copy of this secondary source and it is not clear if any cases supporting Plaintiff's position are cited therein. The court further notes, that if these matters were to be consolidated for discovery only, discovery would track action 602059 and would be closed in both cases as of October 9, 2023. This does not appear to be the outcome sought by the moving party. The parties are welcome to stipulate to the use of discovery obtained in either case to be used in the other matter in order to preserve resources. The Court will not be addressing any such stipulation on this motion as it is not properly before the Court. Late-filed opposition and sur-reply are not considered and are stricken.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
